1404:     }
1405:   }
1406: 
1407:   // -- Query Surrogate Matching (SPECKIT_QUERY_SURROGATES) ----
1408:   //
1409:   // When SPECKIT_QUERY_SURROGATES is enabled, batch-load stored surrogates for
1410:   // all candidate IDs and run matchSurrogates() against each. Candidates with
1411:   // a surrogate match above MIN_MATCH_THRESHOLD receive a score boost (additive,
1412:   // capped at 0.15) to improve ranking for vocabulary-mismatched queries.
1413:   //
1414:   // This is a lightweight post-candidate boost — no new candidates are added,
1415:   // only existing ones are re-scored. No LLM calls on this path.
1416:   //
1417:   // Fail-open: any error leaves candidates unchanged.
1418: 
1419:   if (isQuerySurrogatesEnabled() && candidates.length > 0) {
1420:     try {
1421:       const surrogateDb = requireDb();
1422:       const candidateIds = candidates
1423:         .map((r) => r.id)
1424:         .filter((id): id is number => typeof id === 'number');
1425: 
1426:       if (candidateIds.length > 0) {
1427:         const surrogateMap = loadSurrogatesBatch(surrogateDb, candidateIds);
1428: 
1429:         if (surrogateMap.size > 0) {
1430:           let boostedCount = 0;
1431:           const SURROGATE_BOOST_CAP = 0.15;
1432: 
1433:           candidates = candidates.map((row) => {
1434:             const surrogates = surrogateMap.get(row.id as number);
1435:             if (!surrogates) return row;
1436: 
1437:             const matchResult = matchSurrogates(query, {
1438:               aliases: surrogates.aliases,
1439:               headings: surrogates.headings,
1440:               summary: surrogates.summary,
1441:               surrogateQuestions: surrogates.surrogateQuestions,
1442:               generatedAt: surrogates.generatedAt,
1443:             });
1444: 
1445:             if (matchResult.score > 0) {
1446:               boostedCount++;
1447:               // FIX #2: Use resolveEffectiveScore() as the base instead of
1448:               // raw row.score. For vector-only rows with only `similarity`,
1449:               // row.score may be undefined/0 while similarity is 0.82+.
1450:               // Using the canonical fallback chain prevents overwriting
1451:               // strong relevance signals with tiny surrogate boosts.
1452:               const currentScore = resolveEffectiveScore(row);
1453:               const boost = Math.min(matchResult.score * SURROGATE_BOOST_CAP, SURROGATE_BOOST_CAP);
1454:               const boostedScore = Math.min(1, currentScore + boost);
1455:               return {
1456:                 ...row,
1457:                 score: boostedScore,
1458:                 rrfScore: boostedScore,
1459:                 intentAdjustedScore: boostedScore,
1460:                 surrogateBoost: boost,
1461:                 surrogateMatches: matchResult.matchedSurrogates,
1462:               };
1463:             }
1464: 
1465:             return row;
1466:           });
1467: 
1468:           if (trace && boostedCount > 0) {
1469:             addTraceEntry(trace, 'candidate', 0, boostedCount, 0, {
1470:               channel: 'd2-query-surrogates',
1471:               surrogatesLoaded: surrogateMap.size,
1472:               boostedCount,
1473:             });
1474:           }
1475:         }
1476:       }
1477:     } catch (surrogateErr: unknown) {
1478:       const surrogateMsg = surrogateErr instanceof Error ? surrogateErr.message : String(surrogateErr);
1479:       console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 query surrogate matching failed: ${surrogateMsg}`);
1480:     }
1481:   }
1482: 
1483:   // -- Trace ------------------------------------------------------------------
1484: 
1485:   const durationMs = Date.now() - startTime;
1486: 
1487:   if (trace) {
1488:     addTraceEntry(
1489:       trace,
1490:       'candidate',
1491:       channelCount,
1492:       candidates.length,
1493:       durationMs,
1494:       {
1495:         searchType,
1496:         mode: mode ?? null,
1497:         channelCount,
1498:         deepExpansion: mode === 'deep' && isMultiQueryEnabled(),
1499:         r12EmbeddingExpansion: isEmbeddingExpansionEnabled(),
1500:       }
1501:     );
1502:   }

1504:   // activeChannels counts actual retrieval channels (vector, keyword/BM25),
1505:   // while channelCount counts parallel query variants. In hybrid mode both vector
1506:   // and keyword channels are always active regardless of query variant count.
1507:   const activeChannels = searchType === 'hybrid' ? 2 : 1;

1509:   return {
1510:     candidates,
1511:     metadata: {
1512:       searchType,
1513:       channelCount,
1514:       activeChannels,
1515:       candidateCount: candidates.length,
1516:       constitutionalInjected: constitutionalInjectedCount,
